body.title: Pool semantics: max_instances (singleton = pool-of-1), .0 durable / 1+ ephemeral wiped on startup
body.intention: I intend that a worker class caps its pool at max_instances (singleton = 1) and that instance .0 is durable while 1+ wipe their folder on startup — so a singleton needs no special-casing.
body.description:
**Blocked on: T-shim-lease-instance-numbers** (extends the free-list with a cap + wipe rule). Homogeneity: a singleton is just max_instances=1.

**What to build:**
- A `max_instances` property on the device/class (default e.g. a sensible N; singletons set 1). The class-shim REFUSES to allocate beyond max_instances (returns no slot / the caller queues or Granny gets 'no capacity').
- STARTUP WIPE: instance .0 NEVER wipes its folder (durable foreground/primary); instances 1+ WIPE their assigned ~/.unseen_university/devices/<ABBREV>.<N>/ folder on startup (clean slate).

**Completion criteria:** a class with max_instances=1 (singleton, e.g. Granny) allocates DS.0/Granny.0 and REFUSES a second; a class with max_instances=3 allows .0-.2 and refuses .3; on startup DS.1's folder is wiped, DS.0's is preserved.

**Test plan (real red->green):** unit — set max_instances=1, assert the 2nd allocate is refused; set 3, assert the 4th refused; assert startup wipes DS.1's folder but not DS.0's. Red before the cap/wipe exist; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** Granny (singleton) only ever runs Granny.0; a restarted DS.1 comes up with an empty folder while DS.0 retains prior state. Observable via allocation-refused log + folder contents after startup.

**Logging:** log.info('pool: max_instances=%d reached for <class>, allocation refused') ; log.info('startup: wiped %s (ephemeral)', instance_name) — skipped for .0.

**Observable via:** log.warning('pool: no capacity for <class> (max_instances=1)') + DS.1 folder empty post-startup, DS.0 folder intact.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py (cap in allocate), device.py (max_instances property), instance startup (folder wipe).

**Rollback:** git revert — removes the cap (unbounded pool) + the wipe. No migration.

**Scope boundary:** the cap + wipe only; leasing mechanics are T-shim-lease-instance-numbers.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---

**BUILD SPEC (resolved 2026-07-02 — extends the shipped InstancePool; singleton = max_instances=1, no special case).** Two behaviors: (A) cap, (B) ephemeral-folder wipe.

**Reconciliation (the one fork):** `max_instances` is declared on the device (per D-worker-pool-leasing: "property lives on the device"), BUT the front-door — which enforces the cap by owning the pool — must stay lightweight and MUST NOT import `device.py` (it pulls home_db_url/BaseDevice/CodingCapability = the heavy stack the front-door subprocesses precisely to avoid). Resolution: a tiny lightweight module `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/consts.py` with `MAX_INSTANCES = 1` as the SINGLE source; both the device (`max_instances = MAX_INSTANCES` class attr) and the front-door import it. (DS stays 1 for now — it can't multi-spawn until T-granny-wake; honest, not speculative headroom.)

**File 1 (NEW) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/consts.py`:** `MAX_INSTANCES = 1` + one-line comment (lightweight, NO heavy imports — front-door reads this).

**File 2 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/pool.py`:**
- (A) `InstancePool.__init__` gains `max_instances: int | None = None` (None = unbounded, back-compat with the leasing tests that omit it). In `allocate`: compute `idx = first_free()`; if `self._max_instances is not None and idx >= self._max_instances`: `log.warning("pool: no capacity for %s (max_instances=%d)", class_name, max)` and **return None** (refused — no slot stored, no persist). Otherwise proceed as today and return idx. (So max=1 allows index 0, refuses index 1; max=3 allows 0-2, refuses 3. Singleton = max=1, zero special-casing.)
- (B) module-level `wipe_ephemeral_instance_dir(abbreviation, instance_number, home=None) -> bool`: if `instance_number == 0`: no-op, return False (slot 0 is the DURABLE foreground — NEVER wiped). Else: dir = `<home or uu_home()>/devices/<abbreviation>.<instance_number>/`; if it exists, `shutil.rmtree` it; recreate empty (`mkdir parents=True`); `log.info("startup: wiped %s.%d (ephemeral)", abbreviation, instance_number)`; return True.

**File 3 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/frontdoor.py`:** construct the pool with the cap: `InstancePool("DickSimnel", max_instances=MAX_INSTANCES)` (import MAX_INSTANCES from `.consts`). In `_ensure_device_awake`, after `allocate(...)`: if it returns `None` (refused / no capacity), `log.warning` and return WITHOUT recording — but note: with the slot-0 path and max=1, capacity is only reached if slot 0 is already live, which the earlier `taken()` check already short-circuits, so this is a guard, not a hot path. Do not otherwise change front-door behavior.

**File 4 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/device.py`:** add class attr `max_instances = MAX_INSTANCES` (import from `.consts`). This is the device's own declaration of how many of it may exist (per the decision). Do not otherwise change the device.

**File 5 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/__main__.py`:** the instance-startup wipe. After `device = DickSimnelDevice()` and BEFORE `device._shim.start()`, call `wipe_ephemeral_instance_dir(device.instance_abbreviation, device.instance_number)` (import from `..pool`). So a front-door-spawned DS.1 wipes its folder on boot; standalone/foreground DS.0 (number 0) never does.

**PROOFS (two, one stub→impl pair — the stub neuters BOTH behaviors; two proof_emitter nodes):**
- (A) cap: `tests/test_pool_semantics.py::test_allocate_refused_beyond_max_instances` — `InstancePool("T", max_instances=1, liveness=lambda *a: True, home=tmp)`; allocate once → 0; allocate again → assert **None** (refused). Hollow uncapped build returns 1. Stub: allocate ignores the cap → red (got 1, want None); impl: cap check → None → green.
- (B) wipe: `tests/test_pool_semantics.py::test_ephemeral_dir_wiped_but_zero_preserved` — create `<tmp>/devices/DS.0/marker` and `<tmp>/devices/DS.1/marker`; `wipe_ephemeral_instance_dir("DS", 1, home=tmp)` → assert DS.1/marker GONE and dir exists empty; `wipe_ephemeral_instance_dir("DS", 0, home=tmp)` → assert DS.0/marker PRESERVED and returns False. Hollow no-op-wipe fails the DS.1 arm; hollow always-wipe fails the DS.0 arm. Stub: wipe is a no-op returning False → red (DS.1 marker still present); impl → green.
- Non-proof unit tests same file: max_instances=3 allows 0-2 refuses 3; max=None unbounded (back-compat).

**Scope boundary:** cap + wipe only. Multi-instance SPAWN policy (deciding to actually spawn DS.1) + per-instance mailbox routing are T-granny-wake-not-launch. Leasing mechanics are the (closed) T-shim-lease-instance-numbers. Do NOT generalize max_instances onto BaseDevice yet (homogeneity pass is separate) — DS-local consts is the scope here.

**Operational:** re-run `tests/test_instance_pool.py` + `tests/test_dicksimnel_frontdoor.py` + `tests/test_worker_instance_identity.py` — the cap default (None) must not regress leasing; the front-door pool construction change must not regress the wake proof.

**Affected files (concrete):** NEW `consts.py`, NEW `tests/test_pool_semantics.py`; MODIFY `pool.py`, `frontdoor.py`, `device.py`, `__main__.py`.
body.result: Pool cap + ephemeral wipe shipped (singleton = max_instances=1, no special case). InstancePool.allocate returns None when first_free() >= max_instances; wipe_ephemeral_instance_dir rmtrees+recreates an instance's folder for number>0 (slot 0 durable, never wiped). MAX_INSTANCES lives in a lightweight consts.py both the front-door (caps its pool) and device (class attr) read without importing the heavy device; __main__ wipes on boot. Two proofs: cap-refusal (uncapped build returns 1 not None) + wipe-vs-preserve (no-op build leaves DS.1 intact), both stub->impl red->green. Scope-held: multi-instance spawn policy + per-instance mailbox = T-granny-wake.
